title: rubus glandulosus pallidus
intro: Rubus glandulosus pallidus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Rubus glandulosus pallidus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
